title: Namespaces and qualified names deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Namespaces and qualified names: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? using namespace creates a copy of that namespace's objects and is harmless in public headers.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. A using directive affects name lookup, not object creation; avoid broad directives in headers and prefer qualification or narrow using declarations.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. A using directive affects name lookup, not object creation; avoid broad directives in headers and prefer qualification or narrow using declarations.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: A using directive affects name lookup, not object creation; avoid broad directives in headers and prefer qualification or narrow using declarations.
using namespace creates a copy of that namespace's objects and is harmless in public headers.

answer.explanation: A using directive affects name lookup, not object creation; avoid broad directives in headers and prefer qualification or narrow using declarations.

items.title: Namespaces and qualified names: executable application
items.prompt: Define scale in namespaces metric and binary, read m or b plus an integer, and call the selected function with explicit qualification; metric multiplies by 1000 and binary by 1024.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.
